channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Aaron Judge won't be bothered if Juan Soto gets bigger contract from Yankees than his $360M deal
item.description: Aaron Judge won’t be bothered if Juan Soto gets a bigger deal from the New York Yankees than the captain’s $360 million, nine-year contract.
item.dc:creator: Ronald Blum, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Alyssa Nakken, first full-time female coach in MLB history, leaving Giants to join Guardians
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Alyssa Nakken, the first woman to coach in a Major League Baseball game, is leaving the San Francisco Giants to join the Cleveland Guardians. Nakken made history in 2022 when she took over as first-base coach following an ejection.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Caitlin Clark to join Cincinnati bid for 16th National Women's Soccer League team
item.description: WNBA star Caitlin Clark has joined Cincinnati's bid for a National Women's Soccer League expansion team. Major League Soccer's FC Cincinnati heads the group trying to bring a women's pro team to the city.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lionel Messi's former teammate Javier Mascherano close to becoming Inter Miami coach, AP source says
item.description: Lionel Messi is on the cusp of reuniting with former Argentina national teammate Javier Mascherano with Inter Miami, this time as player and coach, a person with knowledge of the negotiations told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Giants release quarterback Daniel Jones just days after benching him
item.description: EAST RUTHERFORD, N.J. (AP) — The Daniel Jones era in New York is over. The Giants quarterback was granted his release on Friday by the team just days after the franchise said it was benching him in favor of third-stringer Tommy DeVito.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Conor McGregor must pay $250K to woman who says he raped her, civil jury rules
item.description: A civil jury in Ireland has awarded more than $250,000 to a woman who says she was raped by mixed martial arts fighter Conor McGregor in a Dublin hotel penthouse after a night of heavy partying.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: West Coast recruiting: QB flips help USC and Colorado while Cal hangs on (for now) and UCLA readies for a huge night
item.description: On a wild day, the Trojans lost a four-star recruit to Colorado but grabbed a five-star from Texas A&M.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: NBA memo to players urges increased vigilance regarding home security following break-ins
item.description: The NBA is urging its players to take additional precautions to secure their homes following reports of recent high-profile burglaries of dwellings owned by Milwaukee Bucks forward Bobby Portis and Kansas City Chiefs teammates Patrick Mahomes and Travis Kelce.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Boston shuts out Utah Hockey Club, 1-0
item.description: BOSTON (ABC4 Sports) - For the fifth time this season, the Utah Hockey Club failed to score a single goal. Joonas Korpisalo made 21 saves for the Boston Bruins in a 1-0 win against the Utah Hockey Club at TD Garden on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Zach Werenski’s goal in OT and 5-point game helps Blue Jackets to 7-6 win over Lightning
item.description: Zach Werenski’s goal at 1:25 of overtime punctuated a five-point game as the Columbus Blue Jackets rallied from an early three-goal deficit and beat the Tampa Bay Lightning 7-6. Werenski had tied the game at 4 in the second period and added three assists.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE KRAFT, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Brock Purdy's status for the 49ers in doubt this week because of sore shoulder
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — San Francisco 49ers quarterback Brock Purdy has been limited in practice this week because of a sore throwing shoulder and will likely be a game-time decision for Sunday's game at Green Bay.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alex Ovechkin is expected to miss 4 to 6 weeks with a broken left leg
item.description: Alex Ovechkin has a broken left fibula and is expected to be out four to six weeks, an injury that pauses the Washington Capitals superstar captain’s pursuit of Wayne Gretzky’s NHL career goals record.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Teen Paves the Way for Female Hockey Players
item.description: Chloe Jezek, a 16-year-old high school hockey player in North County, is paving the way for other girls in the male-dominated sport.
item.dc:creator: Allison Edmonds, Fox 5

item.title: Cubs trade for Cleveland reliever Eli Morgan; Wisdom DFA'd
item.description: CLEVELAND, Ind. — The Cubs are trading for Cleveland relief pitcher Eli Morgan. As a result of adding Morgan to the 40-man, Patrick Wisdom was designated for assignment. Morgan, 28, had the best season of his career in 2024 with a 1.93 ERA with Cleveland in 42 innings.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: BYU and Arizona State both have exceeded expectations
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU is still in the College Football Playoffs as of now, but they desperately need a win over #21 Arizona State in Tempe this Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jets fire general manager Joe Douglas after team goes 3-8 to start the season: AP source
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York Jets have fired general manager Joe Douglas, a person with knowledge of the decision told The Associated Press on Tuesday. Douglas was in the final year of his contract after being hired in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ticket renewals begin for 2025 Firestone Grand Prix of St. Petersburg
item.description: Fans who purchased 3-Day grandstand tickets in 2024 can now renew their tickets for the Firestone Grand Prix of St. Petersburg.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Zach LaVine's 16 fourth-quarter points lead Bulls past Pistons 122-112
item.description: Zach LaVine scored 16 of his 25 points in the fourth quarter and the Chicago Bulls pulled away for a 122-112 win against the Detroit Pistons on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Another Lakers legend to receive statue outside arena
item.description: One of the winningest coaches in NBA history will forever be enshrined in downtown Los Angeles when the Lakers unveil the newest statue on Star Plaza outside Crypto.com Arena.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: First images of new Ryan Field released: See photos
item.description: EVANSTON, Ill. — Northwestern University and the family that's supported the majority of the funding and planning for the new Ryan Field are billing it as a "sports and entertainment venue set to become a landmark for college football and Chicago." Now, the public can get its first look.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Southwest employee welcomes Chiefs fans by playing Bills song over intercom
item.description: She said she wanted to let Chiefs fans know that "the Buffalo Bills have the best fans in the league.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Minkewicz, NewsNation

item.title: Beyoncé performing at halftime of NFL Christmas Day game on Netflix
item.description: Beyoncé is coming to your home on Christmas — provided you have Netflix and are tuning in to the Baltimore Ravens-Houston Texans game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jazz comeback falls short against Clippers, 116-105
item.description: LOS ANGELES (ABC4 Sports) - James Harden moved into second place on the NBA's career 3-point list and had 20 points and 11 assists to help the Los Angeles Clippers beat the Utah Jazz 116-105 on Sunday night to end a three-game losing streak. Harden moved past Ray Allen with No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Andrei Vasilevskiy makes 29 saves for 36th NHL shutout, Lightning beat Devils 4-0
item.description: Andrei Vasilevskiy made 29 saves for his 36th career shutout to help the Tampa Bay Lightning beat the New Jersey Devils 4-0 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: JOHN KREISER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Draymond Green's foul against Zach Edey is upgraded by the NBA to a flagrant 1
item.description: Golden State forward Draymond Green's foul against Memphis' Zach Edey in the third quarter of the Warriors' victory over the Grizzlies has been upgraded to a category-one flagrant, the NBA said Saturday after reviewing the play.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5
